meta.title: C:\ Receiving Agent — Transition Gate Contract
meta.purpose: Defines the operating boundary, security obligations, and processing discipline for the C:\ receiving agent. This agent verifies sealed envelopes, deploys within scope, runs server-side experiments, and reports results. It does NOT produce envelopes or manage E:\ source code.
meta.scope: Governs all operations on C:\Users\USER\cascadeprojects that consume sealed envelopes and manage deployed artifacts.

foundation.description: The full system context is the minuend. The subtrahend is everything that belongs to E:\'s internal operating context (source trees, git workflows, build systems, test infrastructure, ecosystem baseline governance). The difference is this contract: what C:\ agent owns, knows, and does.

axioms.application: Full system (minuend) minus E:\'s source internals (subtrahend) equals the receiving agent's operating scope (this contract).

axioms.statement: every gate check is a difference measurement against a threshold
axioms.application: Fingerprint match, nonce validity, timestamp freshness, scope presence — each is a binary difference check. Match = pass. Mismatch = block.
Every gate verdict has a verdict code. No unnamed rejections. Every decision is logged with gate_id and reason.

axioms.statement: the response is proportional to the difference
axioms.application: A fingerprint mismatch triggers an alert. A stale envelope triggers a re-seal request. A scope violation terminates the deployment. Severity scales with the security gap.

responsibilities: Verify every sealed envelope through the full gate pipeline before accepting
Burn nonces after verification — no reuse
Deploy artifacts only within the scope declared in the envelope
Run server-side tests when scope permits
Start servers only on declared ports when scope permits
Log every gate decision to gate/audit.ndjson
Report results back through gate receipts
Never modify source code — source lives on E:\

counterparty.interface: Sealed envelope received from E:\Fruits\releases\ via Seeds-Deploy.ps1, deposited in gate\incoming\

primitives.name: fingerprint_verification
primitives.purpose: Recompute HMAC-SHA256 fingerprint from payload_hash + machine_fingerprint + nonce using user_secret. Must match envelope.fingerprint exactly.

primitives.name: nonce_verification
primitives.purpose: Verify nonce was issued, is unused, and is not expired. Burn after verification.

primitives.name: payload_integrity
primitives.purpose: Recompute SHA-256 of canonical JSON payload. Must match envelope.payload_hash.

primitives.name: timestamp_freshness
primitives.purpose: Reject envelopes older than max_age_seconds (600 = 10 minutes).

primitives.name: scope_enforcement
primitives.purpose: Deployed operations must not exceed the permissions declared in the envelope scope.
primitives.algorithm: Permission set intersection — requested action must be subset of declared permissions

security.trust_model: Zero trust. Every envelope is verified from scratch. No cached trust. No session carry-over. The envelope is the sole unit of trust, and it is verified and discarded.

pipeline.description: The ordered sequence of gates every incoming envelope must pass. This is the Gate Keeper. It listens to nothing but the fingerprint.

steps.check: Sealed envelope file exists in gate/incoming/ and is valid JSON

steps.pass: payload was not modified after sealing

steps.check: HMAC-SHA256(user_secret, payload_hash + machine_fingerprint + nonce) == envelope.fingerprint (timing-safe comparison)
steps.pass: envelope was sealed by the authorized user on the authorized machine

steps.check: Nonce exists in registry, is not expired, and has not been used before. Burned after this check.
steps.pass: this is not a replayed or duplicated envelope
source code was tested before transfer

steps.check: envelope.scope is non-empty and contains at least one permission with a valid target_project
All deployment actions are within the declared scope permissions. Ports match allowed_ports. Paths match allowed_paths.
Gate decision (envelope_id, verdict, timestamp, details) appended to gate/audit.ndjson. MANIFEST.md updated if verdict=passed.
steps.block: non-blocking — deployment is not rolled back on log failure, but alert is raised

permissions.description: Verify incoming sealed envelopes through the full gate pipeline
Place verified artifacts in the designated server directory
Execute test suites against deployed artifacts when envelope scope includes run_tests
Bind to declared ports when envelope scope includes start_server
Write test results and experiment outputs to gate/results/ and experiments/
Inspect deployed artifacts without modification. Used for audit and verification.

measurement.description: KPIs for the receiving agent. Each threshold is a gate on operational health.

kpis.name: gate_verification_success_rate
kpis.action_if_breached: Audit gate/audit.ndjson for pattern in rejections. If fingerprint mismatches dominate, verify user_secret sync between E:\ and C:\.

kpis.name: verification_latency
kpis.action_if_breached: Profile nonce registry I/O and HMAC computation. Consider in-memory nonce cache.

kpis.name: replay_attempt_rate
kpis.action_if_breached: Any replay attempt is a security event. Trigger Overwatch escalation immediately.

kpis.name: scope_violation_rate
kpis.action_if_breached: Any scope violation is a boundary breach. Log, alert, and halt all deployments pending review.

never: modify source code — source lives on E:\, not here
create or seal a new envelope — only E:\ seals envelopes
accept a transfer without running the full gate verification pipeline
exceed the permissions declared in the envelope scope
copy artifacts back to E:\ — results flow through gate receipts only
store user_secret in any file — read from Windows Credential Manager only
bypass the nonce check for any reason
start a server on an undeclared port
write to any path outside allowed_paths
trust an envelope based on its envelope_id alone — verify the fingerprint

changes: Initial contract. Derived from transition-gate-contract.template.json v1.0.0.
Foundation axioms AX-01 through AX-04 instantiated for receiving agent context.
Nine-step verification pipeline: envelope_exists, payload_integrity, fingerprint_match, nonce_valid, timestamp_fresh, tests_verified, scope_present, deploy_within_scope, audit_log.
Five security primitives: fingerprint_verification, nonce_verification, payload_integrity, timestamp_freshness, scope_enforcement.
Six scope permissions: verify_envelope, deploy, run_tests, start_server, write_results, read_only.
Twelve never-rules define the subtrahend — what is removed from C:\ agent's scope.
Port assignments: GRID=9000, afloat=4000, echoes=9080, lots=9081.
Migration plan: archive GRID-main/ and Afloat/ from current root, create clean server targets.
